title: Home digital twin inventory and observation family defined
objective: Define the read-only home operating-domain observation family so that every observed home asset carries exactly one identity, one canonical store owner, one telemetry provenance, one digital-twin simulation representation, one privacy boundary, one safe failure mode, and one proof, with the ten observation surfaces named in the goal (discovery, identity, topology, telemetry, digital-twin, resource, media, appliance, network, environment) each pinned to the STORE-001 tiered authority model and the WorldSim USD twin, and with zero physical-device, network, purchase, utility, or credential mutation anywhere in the family.
keystone_decision: generated/store_relationship_decision.json (STORE-001: PostgreSQL+RuVector = canonical durable owner; redb = transient tier + geometry engine; envctl = forward-only bridge; AgentDB = per-agent cognition)
inputs: generated/task_graph.raw.json (LIFEOS-009 parent gate; LPS-029..LPS-036 worldsim ingestion rows)
1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv (canonical vs transient/derived state classification)
mutation_safety_invariant: Every work item in this family is strictly read-only. The family emits zero device-control, network-mutation, purchase, utility-change, or credential-access operations (HOME-001 blocked_paths). Home observation is only the read/parse half of the CodeDB-style ingestion pipeline (sense -> redb buffer -> envctl bridge -> PostgreSQL); it never actuates. Physical control is categorically out of scope for HOME-001 and is deferred to a later gated-physical-control phase per LIFEOS-009 next_action ('decompose home capabilities from read-only observation toward gated physical control'). The WorldSim doctrine from LPS-034 governs the twin: agent code must never mutate the base scan layer.
store_authority_invariant: Per STORE-001: PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single canonical durable owner of the home relational + semantic macro-state (asset identity, ownership, topology graph, durable telemetry history, provenance, embeddings, RuVector GNN edges) as a native No-Sidecar extension. redb is the transient high-frequency tier that buffers discovery/telemetry samples, holds the BLAKE3 dedup cache, performs geometry-engine distance math over twin vectors, and acts as an application-level WAL during PostgreSQL latency/outage. envctl is the forward-only bridge (redb -> embed -> PostgreSQL) and the twin projection engine. No home state class becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class; there is no cloud vector sidecar.
privacy_invariant: All home observation stays inside the local trust boundary: canonical state lives in the local bare-metal PostgreSQL, LifeOS reads via Unix Domain Sockets / shared redb state (blueprint UDS section), and no cloud sidecar ever receives home data. Raw secrets and device credentials are never accessed or copied into PostgreSQL or redb; only references/provenance are stored, with secrets remaining in the OS keyring (STORE-001 external-secret/provenance family, unchanged).

twin_reconciliation.note: HOME-001 next_action requires reconciling LPS-029 through LPS-036. Those existing worldsim ingestion rows ARE the home digital-twin build pipeline; HOME-001 consumes their validated output read-only and does not re-author them.
twin_reconciliation.LPS-029: LiDAR capture of room and workstation (worldsim/scans/*.ply) -> geometry provenance root for the twin (HOME-001-E) and physical-space anchor for discovery (HOME-001-A).
twin_reconciliation.LPS-030: Nix USD toolchain dev shell (usdchecker/usdcat/usdzip + headless MeshLab) -> reproducible tooling that produces every twin proof HOME-001-E cites.
twin_reconciliation.LPS-031: Mesh cleanup to watertight -> the watertight proof consumed by the HOME-001-E failure-mode/proof clauses.
twin_reconciliation.LPS-032: Semantic and instance segmentation (labels.json, per-instance geometry) -> the segmented instances that carry per-asset twin identity (HOME-001-B) and per-room environment mapping (HOME-001-J).
twin_reconciliation.LPS-033: OpenUSD composition with physics schemas (room.usdc, workstation.usdc, materials) -> the twin prim hierarchy used as spatial identity across the family.
twin_reconciliation.LPS-034: Immutable base layer and colliders (stage.usda, UsdPhysics mass/collider) -> the read-only base scan layer doctrine and the appliance/network/media twin prims with authored mass/collider.
twin_reconciliation.LPS-035: Automated twin acceptance gate (four gates: usdchecker zero errors, metric scale, watertight, headless render) -> the acceptance proof that a twin must pass before HOME-001-E may bind assets to it.
twin_reconciliation.LPS-036: Genesis feasibility on validated twin (deterministic headless render) -> optional dynamic-simulation proof for the twin's read-in physical state.

work_items.surface: Read-only discovery of home assets present on the local network and in the scanned physical space (mDNS/DNS-SD and SSDP/UPnP announce listening, passive ARP/neighbor-table read, Zeroconf), producing a point-in-time home asset inventory. No active probing or port scanning that could change or trip device state.
work_items.identity: discovered-endpoint key = (link-layer MAC | stable service-instance name | USD prim path for a physical-space anchor); duplicate announces collapsed by a BLAKE3 content hash of the announce record held in redb (STORE-001 redb dedup-cache role).
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns the canonical asset inventory (STORE-001 assignment 1, durable relational macro-state); the raw scan is buffered first in redb (transient) and bridged forward by envctl. This is the CodeDB ingestion template applied to home discovery, read half only.
work_items.telemetry_provenance: source = local multicast announcements + read-only neighbor table + LiDAR-scanned physical presence (LPS-029); every inventory record carries (source, read-method, observer-node-id, observed_at), timestamped and read-only.
work_items.simulation_representation: each discovered physical asset binds to a USD prim in the WorldSim twin (stage.usda, LPS-034) when it has a spatial footprint; network-only assets bind to a HOME-001-C topology-graph node.
work_items.privacy_boundary: inventory is sensitive-personal (MACs and service names are PII-adjacent); stays inside the local trust boundary (local PostgreSQL, No-Sidecar, UDS); never exported to any cloud sidecar.
work_items.failure_mode: on discovery failure or PostgreSQL outage, redb WAL buffers the partial scan; assets not seen are marked stale/unknown, never assumed absent or removed; discovery never retries by probing or mutating a device.
work_items.proof: PostgreSQL asset-registry row count reconciles with the redb buffer forward-flush receipt; every inventory row has a non-null provenance tuple; the command log contains zero device-write operations (mutation-free proof).
work_items.no_physical_mutation: passive/announce-listening only; no active probe, no port scan, no device write.
work_items.decision: Discovery is the read-only ingestion front door; the canonical inventory is owned exactly once by PostgreSQL, buffered transiently in redb, and bridged forward-only by envctl.

work_items.surface: A single canonical identity record per observed home asset that unifies its network identity, hardware identity, and spatial (twin) identity, and links it to an owning person reference.
work_items.identity: composite stable key {hardware_id (MAC/serial where read-only visible), service_instance, usd_prim_path, first_seen, owner_person_ref}; identity lineage preserved from the originating discovery announce hash (STORE-001 invariant 'provenance preserved').
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (STORE-001 assignment 1) is the single canonical identity owner exactly once; identity is the join key used by topology, telemetry, resource, media, appliance, network, and environment surfaces.
work_items.telemetry_provenance: identity is assembled from HOME-001-A discovery records plus the HOME-001-E twin prim binding; it uses only already-broadcast or read-only-visible identifiers. No credential is read (HOME-001 blocked_paths: credential access).
work_items.simulation_representation: the identity row references its twin prim path (LPS-033/LPS-034) so the registry and the twin share one spatial identity.
work_items.privacy_boundary: identity ties assets to persons -> highest privacy tier; person references are local opaque refs; raw device secrets are never stored (STORE-001 external-secret family stays in the OS keyring).
work_items.failure_mode: an identity conflict (two assets resolving to one key) is quarantined as 'unresolved-identity', never silently overwritten; ambiguity yields no action.
work_items.proof: every asset row has a non-null identity key, owner reference, and provenance lineage; a uniqueness constraint proves one record per asset; a keyring audit proves zero home-device secrets were copied into PostgreSQL.
work_items.no_physical_mutation: registry assembly is a pure read/join; no device is contacted for identity beyond passive discovery.
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL owns home-asset identity exactly once; identity is the single join key across the entire family.

work_items.surface: The canonical simulation representation of the physical home: the WorldSim USD twin (worldsim/usd/room.usdc, workstation.usdc, stage.usda) with an immutable read-only base scan layer, built by the existing LPS-029..LPS-036 ingestion pipeline.
work_items.identity: twin prim path in the LPS-033/LPS-034 UsdGeom hierarchy is the spatial identity that HOME-001-B joins on; the base-scan .usdc is the read-only defaultPrim sublayered at the bottom of the layer stack (LPS-034 doctrine: agent code must never mutate the base scan layer).
work_items.owner: twin geometry is authored and immutable under worldsim/ (LPS-029..LPS-034); the asset<->twin-prim binding and twin metadata are canonical in PostgreSQL (STORE-001 assignment 1); the twin is projected/materialized read-only by envctl (STORE-001 assignment 5, derived projection); redb is the geometry engine performing f32 distance math over twin vectors (blueprint 'high-speed geometry engine').
work_items.telemetry_provenance: geometry provenance = LiDAR capture (LPS-029) -> mesh repair (LPS-031) -> segmentation (LPS-032) -> USD composition (LPS-033); dynamic-state provenance is read-in only from HOME-001-D telemetry and is never written back to a device.
work_items.simulation_representation: this surface IS the simulation representation for the whole family; every physical asset in A/B/F/G/H/I/J binds to a prim here.
work_items.privacy_boundary: the twin is a precise interior map of the home -> highest spatial-privacy tier; base scans and stage never leave the local trust boundary; no cloud rendering.
work_items.failure_mode: a twin that fails the LPS-035 four-gate acceptance (usdchecker zero errors, metric scale, watertight, headless render) is rejected, not used; all overrides live in upper layers so the base truth is never corrupted (LPS-034 layer-stack invariant); acting in the twin never actuates a physical device.
work_items.proof: worldsim/reports/twin_validation.json four-gate green (LPS-035) + usdchecker exit 0 (LPS-033) + watertight proof (LPS-031) + immutable-base layer-stack diff (LPS-034) + optional deterministic Genesis render (LPS-036).
work_items.no_physical_mutation: the twin is a simulation; all writes land in upper USD layers, never on the base scan and never on a device.
work_items.decision: The WorldSim USD twin is the single simulation representation for HOME-001; its base layer is immutable, its binding/metadata canonical in PostgreSQL, its distance math in redb. Reconciling LPS-029..LPS-036 here closes the HOME-001 next_action.

work_items.surface: A read-only graph of how home assets connect (network adjacency: device <-> AP <-> switch <-> router <-> WAN) and where they sit (spatial adjacency from the twin: room containment, surface support, power source).
work_items.identity: edge = (src_identity, dst_identity, relation in {connected_via, contained_in, supported_by, powered_from}); nodes are HOME-001-B identities.
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector native graph/GNN (STORE-001 assignment 2, semantic/graph state, No-Sidecar); the spatial half is the same identities as the twin scene graph (one graph, not a sidecar).
work_items.telemetry_provenance: network edges from read-only neighbor/bridge tables and observed announce paths; spatial edges from segmented twin instances (LPS-032) and colliders (LPS-034); every edge carries its provenance tuple, read-only.
work_items.simulation_representation: spatial edges ARE the twin's scene-graph hierarchy (stage.usda); network edges are a RuVector graph layer over the same asset identities.
work_items.privacy_boundary: topology reveals home layout and connectivity -> sensitive; the RuVector graph lives in the local PostgreSQL, never a cloud graph service.
work_items.failure_mode: a stale edge is marked with last-seen; an inconsistent subgraph is isolated (RuVector MinCut doctrine) rather than trusted; the physical network is never reconfigured (blocked: network mutation).
work_items.proof: every edge references two valid HOME-001-B identities; the spatial half validates against the usdchecker-clean twin scene graph (LPS-035); zero network-config write operations are logged.
work_items.no_physical_mutation: graph is derived from read-only tables and the read-only twin; no routing or link change is issued.
work_items.decision: Topology is canonical in PostgreSQL+RuVector as one graph; its spatial half shares the twin's identities (No-Sidecar), its network half is read-only-derived.

work_items.surface: High-frequency read-only state and sensor samples from assets that expose read APIs or passive signals (power-draw ticks, on/off state flags, link throughput, occupancy).
work_items.identity: sample key = (asset_identity, metric, observed_at); identical consecutive samples collapsed by BLAKE3 dedup in redb (STORE-001 dedup-cache role).
work_items.owner: redb is the first-tier transient high-frequency buffer (STORE-001 assignment 3); envctl forward-flushes only settled/significant states; PostgreSQL owns the durable telemetry history. Raw high-frequency stream is transient by definition.
work_items.telemetry_provenance: the literal telemetry-provenance the gate demands is the (source-device/sensor, read-method, observer-node, observed_at) tuple stamped on every sample; all reads are read-only API or passive signal.
work_items.simulation_representation: telemetry drives the twin's dynamic read-in state (e.g., an appliance prim's rigid-body/collider state per LPS-034) but is never written back to a device.
work_items.privacy_boundary: occupancy and power patterns are behaviorally revealing -> sensitive; the raw stream stays in local redb (process lifecycle); only aggregated significant states persist to PostgreSQL; no cloud telemetry sink.
work_items.failure_mode: on PostgreSQL latency/outage, redb acts as the application-level WAL (blueprint redb section), buffering until sync resumes; on sensor dropout the gap is marked, never interpolated into an action.
work_items.proof: redb -> envctl forward-flush receipt; PostgreSQL durable rows carry the full provenance tuple; the transient raw stream is expendable by design; zero control commands are emitted from the telemetry path.
work_items.no_physical_mutation: sampling only; the telemetry path has no write channel to any device.
work_items.decision: Telemetry is redb's intended transient terminal role with forward-only flush of significant states to PostgreSQL, matching STORE-001 assignment 3 exactly.

work_items.surface: Read-only meters for home resources: electrical power/energy, water, gas, internet bandwidth, and local storage capacity.
work_items.identity: meter key = (resource_type, meter_identity, observed_at); meter_identity is a HOME-001-B registry entry.
work_items.owner: high-frequency meter ticks buffer in redb (STORE-001 assignment 3); durable resource history and budgets are owned by PostgreSQL (STORE-001 assignment 1).
work_items.telemetry_provenance: read-only utility-meter API, smart-plug read, or router bandwidth counter; every reading carries (source-meter, read-method, observer, observed_at). No utility setting or tariff is changed (blocked: utility change).
work_items.simulation_representation: resource flows annotate twin prims as read-in state (e.g., a workstation prim's power draw) and form a resource-flow graph layer in RuVector.
work_items.privacy_boundary: consumption patterns reveal presence and behavior -> sensitive; local-only aggregation; raw ticks are transient in redb.
work_items.failure_mode: a meter dropout marks a gap with no estimated-billing action; a PostgreSQL outage falls back to the redb WAL; the family never changes a utility setting, tariff, or valve.
work_items.proof: durable resource rows carry the provenance tuple; the redb -> envctl flush receipt is recorded; zero utility-write operations are logged.
work_items.no_physical_mutation: metering is read-only; no valve, breaker, or tariff is touched.
work_items.decision: Resource observation is read-only metering with redb transient and PostgreSQL durable ownership; no utility mutation.

work_items.surface: Read-only observation of media servers/libraries (catalog metadata) and playback-device state (which device, idle/playing) - a catalog inventory, not content exfiltration.
work_items.identity: media_asset key = (server_identity | player_identity, catalog_item_ref, usd_prim_path for a physical player); registry entries in HOME-001-B.
work_items.owner: durable media-asset registry and catalog metadata are owned by PostgreSQL + RuVector (semantic search over the catalog); transient playback state flags buffer in redb.
work_items.telemetry_provenance: read-only media-server catalog API read and player-state read; every record carries its provenance tuple. No media is purchased (blocked: purchase).
work_items.simulation_representation: physical players (TV, speaker) bind to twin prims; the catalog is a metadata layer, not geometry.
work_items.privacy_boundary: media consumption is highly personal -> sensitive; only catalog metadata and references are stored locally; content itself is never copied (mirrors STORE-001 'references/provenance, never raw').
work_items.failure_mode: an offline media server marks its catalog stale; the family never starts/stops playback or purchases content.
work_items.proof: catalog rows carry provenance; no purchase or playback-control command is emitted; player state is transient in redb.
work_items.no_physical_mutation: no playback control, no purchase, no content write.
work_items.decision: Media observation stores catalog metadata and player state read-only; content and purchases are excluded by the blocked-paths contract.

work_items.surface: Read-only state of home appliances that expose read APIs (HVAC setpoint/mode read, fridge temperature, washer cycle state) - observe, never actuate.
work_items.identity: appliance key = (appliance_identity, usd_prim_path, read_only_capability_profile); registry in HOME-001-B; twin prim per LPS-034 (static appliance collider or rigid body with authored mass).
work_items.owner: durable appliance registry and significant state history are owned by PostgreSQL; high-frequency state flags buffer in redb.
work_items.telemetry_provenance: read-only appliance API or local-hub read; every record carries its provenance tuple; the capability profile records only readable attributes. No device control (blocked: device control).
work_items.simulation_representation: each appliance binds to a twin prim with UsdPhysics mass and collider authored (LPS-034); appliance state is read-in twin state only.
work_items.privacy_boundary: appliance usage reveals routines -> sensitive; local-only; state is read via an already-authorized local hub, storing references only, never credentials.
work_items.failure_mode: an unreachable appliance is marked stale/unknown; the family never issues a setpoint, mode, or command (safe-by-omission).
work_items.proof: appliance rows carry provenance and a read-only capability profile; the command log has zero control commands; the twin prim's authored mass/collider is proven by LPS-034.
work_items.no_physical_mutation: no setpoint, mode, cycle, or power command is ever issued.
work_items.decision: Appliances are observed read-only with an explicitly read-only capability profile; control is categorically excluded and deferred to a later gated-physical-control phase (LIFEOS-009 next_action).

work_items.surface: Read-only state of network infrastructure: router/AP/switch device state, WAN link up/down and throughput, DHCP lease-table read, and Wi-Fi client-list read.
work_items.identity: network_device key = (device_identity, role in {router, ap, switch, modem}, usd_prim_path for physical gear); registry in HOME-001-B; edges feed the HOME-001-C topology.
work_items.owner: durable network-device registry and config-snapshot provenance are owned by PostgreSQL; high-frequency link counters buffer in redb.
work_items.telemetry_provenance: read-only management API / SNMP-read / lease-table read; every snapshot carries its provenance tuple. No network mutation (blocked: network mutation) - no config write, reboot, or firewall change.
work_items.simulation_representation: physical network gear binds to twin prims; logical link state feeds the RuVector topology graph (HOME-001-C).
work_items.privacy_boundary: client lists reveal who and what is home -> highly sensitive; local-only; reads use an already-authorized local admin read session and secrets remain in the OS keyring (STORE-001 external-secret family).
work_items.failure_mode: an unreachable device marks its link down/stale; the family never reconfigures the network; on inconsistency the subgraph is isolated (MinCut) rather than trusted.
work_items.proof: network rows carry provenance and a read-only snapshot; zero config-write or reboot operations are logged; topology edges validate against HOME-001-C.
work_items.no_physical_mutation: no config, reboot, firewall, or channel change is ever issued.
work_items.decision: Network is observed read-only; all mutation (config/reboot/firewall) is categorically excluded per blocked-paths and credentials are never accessed.

work_items.surface: Read-only environmental sensors: temperature, humidity, air quality (CO2/PM2.5), ambient light, sound level, and occupancy.
work_items.identity: env_sample key = (sensor_identity, metric, observed_at); sensor registry in HOME-001-B; each sensor bound to a twin prim location.
work_items.owner: high-frequency environment samples buffer in redb (STORE-001 assignment 3); durable environment history and aggregates are owned by PostgreSQL.
work_items.telemetry_provenance: read-only sensor read (local hub / Zigbee / Matter read); every sample carries (sensor, read-method, observer, observed_at); passive.
work_items.simulation_representation: environment fields annotate twin regions - per-room readings mapped onto LPS-032 segmented instances - forming a scalar-field layer over the twin.
work_items.privacy_boundary: occupancy and sound reveal presence -> sensitive; the raw stream is transient in redb; only aggregates are persisted; no cloud sink.
work_items.failure_mode: a sensor dropout marks a gap with no HVAC or vent action (observation only); a PostgreSQL outage falls back to the redb WAL (safe-by-omission).
work_items.proof: durable environment rows carry the provenance tuple; the redb -> envctl flush receipt is recorded; zero actuation commands are logged; readings map to valid LPS-032 twin segments.
work_items.no_physical_mutation: sampling only; no HVAC, vent, humidifier, or light is actuated.
work_items.decision: Environment observation is read-only sampling with redb transient and PostgreSQL durable aggregates; no environmental actuation.

completion_gate: Each of the ten observation surfaces (HOME-001-A..J) carries a non-empty identity, owner, telemetry_provenance, simulation_representation, privacy_boundary, failure_mode, and proof; every surface is read-only with an explicit no_physical_mutation statement and zero device/network/purchase/utility/credential operations; the digital-twin surface (HOME-001-E) reconciles LPS-029 through LPS-036; every owner assignment traces to a STORE-001 state_class_assignment; and HOME-001.proof.json records product_execution_performed=false with STORE-001 alignment.
